title: Express Middleware, Promise Errors, Dependencies, and Response Ownership · Narrowing Workshop
summary: A backend pairing room predicts runtime, request, middleware, dependency, response, and cleanup transitions before assembling the first composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary for express middleware, promise errors, dependencies, and response ownership. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of composed middleware, dependency, and error boundary while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, caus…
objectives: Compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order.
Use Express 5 automatic Promise rejection forwarding while preserving causal error identity and avoiding duplicate next calls.
Implement four-argument error middleware, normalized failure taxonomy, disclosure policy, and headersSent delegation without double writes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Middleware Order Short Circuit
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop team handles case 9289 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to trace defended middleware order while keeping error and response ownership explicit; survive a dependency slowdown without queue growth; then saturate one tenant budget while another tenant remains serviceable. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Middleware Order Short Circuit.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for compose request identity, trust, limits, parsing, authentication, authorization, application, error, and observation middleware in a defended order. For Node and Express server Commit to the mission model · Workshop Middleware Order Short Circuit, keep Middleware Order Short Circuit connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate transport parsing, domain decisions, persistence, external effects, and serialization while assigning exactly one response owner.
